rationale.root_cause: At the bad sink (around line 42), memcpy uses sizeof(structCharVoid) to copy into structCharVoid.charFirst (16 bytes), overrunning the subobject and overwriting voidSecond/voidThird. This corrupts voidSecond so the later printLine dereferences a bogus pointer and crashes.
rationale.local_fix: Change the memcpy length to sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst) instead of sizeof(structCharVoid). This bounds the copy to the destination buffer size.
rationale.global_fix: Encapsulate writes to charFirst behind a bounded API (e.g., copy_charFirst(charVoid*, const char*) using sizeof dst->charFirst) so the copy length is tied to the field’s capacity. This represents the destination as a bounded buffer and prevents type-overrun misuse.
